Description: Whoever, intending to take dishonestly any movable property out of the possession of any person without that person's consent, moves that property in order to such taking, is said to commit theft.
Positive_Precedent: State vs. Ravi (2024): Accused moved the bike without consent, found guilty even though he returned it later.
Negative_Precedent: Kumar vs. State (2023): Accused took the umbrella by mistake believing it was his own. Acquitted due to lack of dishonest intention.

Description: A person commits murder if the act by which the death is caused is done with the intention of causing death, or causing bodily injury sufficient to cause death.
Positive_Precedent: City Prosecutor vs. John (2022): Stab wound to the heart proved intention to kill. Convicted.
Negative_Precedent: State vs. Meera (2024): Death caused by accidental push during a verbal argument. Ruled as Culpable Homicide, not Murder.

Description: Whoever, being in any manner entrusted with property, or with any dominion over property, dishonestly misappropriates or converts to his own use that property.
Positive_Precedent: Bank vs. Manager (2023): Manager diverted customer funds to personal account. Convicted.
Negative_Precedent: Company vs. Employee (2022): Employee lost company laptop due to negligence, not dishonesty. Civil liability only.

Description: Whoever, by deceiving any person, fraudulently or dishonestly induces the person so deceived to deliver any property to any person.
Positive_Precedent: Victim vs. Scammer (2024): Accused promised a job that did not exist and took money. Convicted.
Negative_Precedent: Buyer vs. Seller (2023): Product was defective but seller did not know. No deception proved.
